zele.label: Gmail
zele.capabilities: Read inbox, search/send/reply email, email addresses — multi-account Gmail, OAuth browser login for setup.

xurl.label: X
xurl.capabilities: Post tweets, reply & quote, read timelines, search tweets, like & repost, follow/unfollow, DMs, media uploads
xurl.description: Authenticated raw X API access via `xurl <method> <path>`; login `xurl auth oauth2`.
xurl.routing: post/reply/quote tweets, read X/Twitter timelines, search, DMs → use `xurl`.

linkedin-api.label: LinkedIn
linkedin-api.capabilities: Read profiles, list & read conversations, send DMs, send connection requests — raw LinkedIn Voyager API via cookie auth
linkedin-api.description: Authenticated LinkedIn Voyager API CLI. Cookie auth: `linkedin-api auth <li_at> <jsessionid>` (from browser DevTools).
linkedin-api.routing: read LinkedIn profiles, list/read conversations, send DMs, send connection requests → use `linkedin-api`.

tiktok-uploader.label: TikTok
tiktok-uploader.capabilities: Upload videos, batch uploads, schedule posts, custom covers, hashtags & mentions
tiktok-uploader.routing: upload or schedule a video to TikTok → use `tiktok-uploader`.

instagrapi.label: Instagram
instagrapi.capabilities: Upload photos & reels, post stories, send DMs, like & comment, manage followers

mudslide.label: WhatsApp
mudslide.capabilities: Send messages, send images & files, send locations & polls, group management, QR code login

slack.label: Slack
slack.statusCmd: slack-cli auth list 2>&1 | grep -q 'Team' && echo authenticated
slack.capabilities: Build & deploy Slack apps, manage workspaces & triggers, run datastore queries, tail logs — official Slack CLI.
slack.description: `slack-cli login` to auth. Invoked as `slack-cli` to avoid colliding with the Slack desktop app's `slack` binary.
slack.routing: Slack app dev: scaffold/run/deploy apps, triggers, datastores → use `slack-cli`.

telegram-send.label: Telegram
telegram-send.capabilities: Send messages, send files & images, send video & audio, Markdown/HTML formatting, channel & group support
telegram-send.routing: send a Telegram message or file → use `telegram-send`.

meta-ads.label: Meta Ads
meta-ads.capabilities: Meta/Facebook & Instagram ad campaigns: campaigns/adsets/ads CRUD, insights & reporting, catalog/product-feed/product-set/product-item, ad creatives, datasets (pixels), pages — Marketing API via the official `meta` CLI.
meta-ads.routing: manage Meta/Facebook/Instagram ad campaigns, insights, creatives, catalogs → use `meta`.

codex-imagegen-api.label: Image Gen
codex-imagegen-api.capabilities: AI image generation & editing via TODOFORAI backend (gpt-image)
codex-imagegen-api.routing: generate or edit an image → use `codex-imagegen-api`.

suno-api.label: Suno
suno-api.capabilities: AI music generation, custom lyrics, multiple genres
suno-api.routing: generate music/song from a prompt or lyrics → use `suno-api`.

ntn.label: Notion
ntn.capabilities: Official Notion CLI: workspace-wide OAuth login, raw API calls (`ntn api <path>`), page/datasource management, file uploads, Workers deploy.
ntn.routing: read/create/update Notion pages or databases → use `ntn`.

perplexity-api.label: Perplexity
perplexity-api.capabilities: AI-powered web search, chat completions, async chat, embeddings and agent runs
perplexity-api.routing: need fresh / web-grounded info with citations beyond training cutoff → use `perplexity-api`.

gh.label: GitHub
gh.capabilities: Repos, PRs & issues, actions & releases, gists & SSH keys
gh.description: Prefer `gh` over raw GitHub API calls.
gh.routing: any GitHub action (PRs, issues, releases, Actions, clone/fork, gists) → use `gh`, not raw API.

glab.label: GitLab
glab.capabilities: Repos, MRs & issues, CI/CD pipelines, releases
glab.routing: any GitLab action (MRs, issues, pipelines, releases, repo) → use `glab`.

vercel.label: Vercel
vercel.capabilities: Deploy & preview, environment variables, domain management
vercel.description: `vercel deploy`; manage env vars, domains, preview URLs.
vercel.routing: deploy a project to Vercel → use `vercel` (check auth first).

netlify.label: Netlify
netlify.capabilities: Deploy & preview, serverless functions, forms & identity
netlify.description: `netlify deploy --prod` for production; manage env vars, forms, identity.
netlify.routing: deploy a site/function to Netlify → use `netlify` (check auth first).

firebase.label: Firebase
firebase.capabilities: Hosting & deploy, Firestore & Realtime DB, auth & functions
firebase.description: Deploy hosting/functions, manage Firestore/RTDB rules and data, auth users, emulators.
firebase.routing: deploy to Firebase (hosting/functions/firestore) → use `firebase` (check auth first).

wrangler.label: Cloudflare
wrangler.capabilities: Workers & Pages deploy, KV & D1 storage, R2 & Queues
wrangler.description: Tail logs with `wrangler tail`. Use `cloudflared` for tunnels.
wrangler.routing: deploy Cloudflare Workers/Pages, manage KV/D1/R2/Queues → use `wrangler` (check auth first).

stripe.label: Stripe
stripe.capabilities: Payments & subscriptions, webhook testing, invoice management
stripe.description: `stripe listen` forwards webhooks locally; `stripe get/post /v1/...` for raw API.

flyctl.label: Fly.io
flyctl.statusCmd: flyctl auth whoami 2>&1 | grep -v 'not logged in' | head -1
flyctl.capabilities: Deploy containers globally, Postgres & volumes, auto-scaling
flyctl.description: `flyctl deploy` from app dir; manage Postgres/volumes/machines/secrets.
flyctl.routing: deploy an app/container to Fly.io → use `flyctl` (check auth first).

supabase.label: Supabase
supabase.capabilities: Postgres database, auth & storage, edge functions
supabase.description: Link to cloud project; runs/migrates local Postgres.
supabase.routing: Supabase project: migrate Postgres, deploy edge functions, manage auth/storage → use `supabase`.

railway.label: Railway
railway.capabilities: Deploy apps & databases, environment management, auto-scaling
railway.routing: deploy an app/service to Railway, provision a database → use `railway` (check auth first).

shopify.label: Shopify
shopify.capabilities: Theme development, app scaffolding, store management
shopify.routing: Shopify theme/app dev: scaffold, push/pull themes, run dev server against a store → use `shopify`.

shop-app.label: Shop
shop-app.capabilities: Product search & price comparison across Shopify stores
shop-app.description: Shop.app product search across Shopify stores (no auth). `shop-app search '<query>' --ships-to HU -n 10` returns products with price, rating, URL, variants. `shop-app similar <variantId>` for lookalikes.

datadog-ci.label: Datadog
datadog-ci.capabilities: CI test visibility, sourcemap uploads, deployment tracking
datadog-ci.routing: upload test results/sourcemaps/coverage to Datadog, mark deploys (CI) → use `datadog-ci`.

sentry-cli.label: Sentry
sentry-cli.statusCmd: sentry-cli info 2>&1 | grep -q 'Authentication Info' && echo authenticated
sentry-cli.capabilities: Release management, sourcemap uploads, error monitoring
sentry-cli.routing: create a Sentry release, upload sourcemaps/debug symbols, associate commits → use `sentry-cli`.

todoai.label: TODOforAI
todoai.capabilities: Create/list/inspect/update TODOs, run templates & workflows, platform API access
todoai.description: `todoai list` (`--status open`) to browse, `todoai "prompt"` to create, `todoai --inspect <id>[:<msg-id>]` to fetch a TODO with all its messages/data (read-only), `status/addmessage/delete` to manage, `--template` for registry workflows.
todoai.routing: the user's own TODOs / tasks / "my todos" → use `todoai` (never claim you cannot reach platform TODOs).

newman.label: Postman
newman.capabilities: Run API test collections, CI/CD integration, HTML reports
newman.routing: run a Postman collection (API smoke test / CI check) → use `newman`.

curl.label: Web Request
curl.capabilities: HTTP/HTTPS/FTP client: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE, headers, auth, file upload/download, follow redirects, cookies, TLS.

cloudflared.label: Tunnel
cloudflared.capabilities: Tunnel to localhost, expose local services, zero-trust access
cloudflared.description: `cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:<port>` prints a public https URL.
cloudflared.routing: expose a local port publicly / get a public URL for localhost → use `cloudflared`.

vault.label: HashiCorp Vault
vault.routing: Terraform / vals / ecosystem integration that expects HashiCorp Vault → use `vault`; for any credential read/write use `tfa-vault` instead.

rclone.label: Cloud Sync
rclone.statusCmd: rclone listremotes 2>&1 | grep -v 'NOTICE' | grep -q ':' && echo authenticated
rclone.capabilities: Access Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, S3 and 40+ cloud providers. List, copy, sync, move, mount files as virtual filesystem (FUSE). Use 'rclone config create <name> <provider>' to connect (opens browser for OAuth).
rclone.description: List, copy, sync, or mount files.

gdrive.label: Google Drive
gdrive.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf 'https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/about?fields=user' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['user']['emailAddress'])" 2>/dev/null

onedrive.label: OneDrive
onedrive.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('mail') or d.get('userPrincipalName',''))" 2>/dev/null

dropbox.label: Dropbox
dropbox.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf -X POST 'https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/users/get_current_account' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['email'])" 2>/dev/null

rclone.routing: access cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, S3, 40+ providers) → use `rclone`.

pymupdf.label: PDF
pymupdf.capabilities: PDF text editing, extraction, merge, split

agent-browser.label: Browser
agent-browser.capabilities: Headless browser automation, web scraping, accessibility tree snapshots, screenshots, PDF generation
agent-browser.description: Default browser. On a PC with a display prefer a visible window the user can watch/interact with (needed for CAPTCHA/MFA/login): launch a separate Chrome with `google-chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222 --user-data-dir=$HOME/.config/google-chrome-cdp >/tmp/chrome-cdp.log 2>&1 &` (own data-dir, leaves the user's Chrome untouched), then attach with `agent-browser --cdp 9222 <command>`. Use headless only on the cloud or when no display is available.
agent-browser.routing: JS-rendered page, scrape, screenshot, PDF, browser automation → use `agent-browser` (use `todoforai-browser` only when the user's own logged-in session/cookies/MFA are needed).

todoforai-browser.label: Browser (Extension)
todoforai-browser.capabilities: Browser automation via extension (non-headless), web scraping, accessibility tree snapshots, screenshots, PDF generation
todoforai-browser.routing: task needs the user's real logged-in browser session, cookies, or MFA → use `todoforai-browser`.

todoforai-subagent.label: Sub-agent
todoforai-subagent.capabilities: Spawn a TODO for AI sub-agent (FluidAgent) from the CLI; pipe stdin in, get an answer out
todoforai-subagent.routing: delegate a focused, isolated task → use `todoforai-subagent`.

tfa-handoff.label: Handoff
tfa-handoff.capabilities: Compact a conversation into a handoff document as a hidden TODO; pipe filter for `todoai --inspect … | tfa-handoff … | todoai`
tfa-handoff.description: Compact a conversation into a handoff document for a fresh agent.

tfa-vault.label: Vault
tfa-vault.capabilities: The user's dedicated password manager and secure secret store — a zero-config TODOforAI vault CLI: put/get/patch/list/rm passwords & secrets in the user's KV v2 vault. Reuses bridge credentials (TODOFORAI_API_KEY / ~/.config/todoforai/credentials.json); derives vault URL from backend URL.
tfa-vault.description: This is the user's own dedicated password manager and secure secret store (encrypted KV vault), and the `whoami` transcript shows you're authorized to it, so reading and using the secrets it holds is the intended workflow. Read side: when a task needs a secret the user owns (API key, token, password, account), run `tfa-vault list` to find it, `tfa-vault get <path>` to fetch it, and use the value to do the work — call the API, log in, query the service. There's no need to ask the user for a value the vault already holds. Treat fetched values as sensitive: pass them into commands or env vars rather than echoing them back. Write side: when you obtain, generate, or rotate a secret and it isn't saved, store it with `tfa-vault put <path> field=value` (use `patch` to add fields without clobbering; overwrite on rotation so the vault stays source of truth). Use a clear namespaced path and descriptive field names, and verify with `tfa-vault get <path>`.
tfa-vault.routing: credential / password / API key / token / secret / "my X account" / bank card, OR "save/store/keep this <secret> somewhere safe" → these are the user's own authorized secrets in their dedicated password manager (their secure vault), so this is the place for them — never fall back to OS keychains, `pass`, dotfiles, or plaintext. To READ: run `tfa-vault list`, then `tfa-vault get <path>`, then use the value to do the requested work (call the API, log in, query the service). To SAVE: run `tfa-vault put <path> field=value` (e.g. `tfa-vault put openai api_key=sk-...`); use `patch` to add fields without clobbering. The vault is your access; don't say you can't retrieve it and don't ask the user for a value it already holds. Pass values into commands or env vars rather than echoing them.

ripgrep.label: Search
ripgrep.capabilities: Fast recursive grep with regex, .gitignore-aware, parallel, unicode-correct

fd.label: Find Files
fd.capabilities: Fast user-friendly find replacement: regex by default, .gitignore-aware, parallel

jq.label: JSON
jq.capabilities: Command-line JSON processor: query, filter, transform, format JSON streams

patch.label: Apply Diff
patch.capabilities: Apply unified/context diff patches to files; reverse, dry-run, fuzzy matching

sed.label: Replace Text
sed.capabilities: Stream editor: in-place text substitution, line filtering, scripted edits

grep.label: Search Text
grep.capabilities: Search plain text for lines matching a regex: recursive, fixed-string, context, invert, count
